children.heading: .—State apportionment
children.label: 2 .—State apportionment
children.text: On October 1 of fiscal year 2022 and each fiscal year thereafter, the Secretary shall apportion the sums authorized to be appropriated for expenditure on the national highway performance program under section 119, the surface transportation block grant program under section 133, the highway safety improvement program under section 148, the congestion mitigation and air quality improvement program under section 149, the national highway freight program under section 167, the carbon reduction program under section 175, to carry out subsection (c) of the PROTECT program under section 176, and to carry out section 134 in accordance with paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: i .—In general
children.text: Except as provided in clause (ii), the amounts apportioned to a State under subsection (b)(6) shall be made available by the State to the metropolitan planning organizations responsible for carrying out section 134 in the State.

children.heading: .—States receiving minimum apportionment
children.label: ii .—States receiving minimum apportionment
children.text: A State that received the minimum apportionment for use in carrying out section 134 for fiscal year 2009 may, subject to the approval of the Secretary, use the funds apportioned under subsection (b)(6) to fund transportation planning outside of urbanized areas.

children.heading: .—Unused funds
children.label: B .—Unused funds
children.text: Any funds that are not used to carry out section 134 may be made available by a metropolitan planning organization to the State to fund activities under section 135.

children.heading: .—Reimbursement
children.label: B .—Reimbursement
children.text: Not later than 15 business days after the date of receipt by a State of a request for reimbursement of expenditures made by a metropolitan planning organization for carrying out section 134, the State shall reimburse, from amounts distributed under this paragraph to the metropolitan planning organization by the State, the metropolitan planning organization for those expenditures.

children.heading: .—Determination of population figures
children.label: 3 .—Determination of population figures

children.heading: .—Notice to states
children.label: 2 .—Notice to states
children.text: If the Secretary has not made an apportionment under this section for a fiscal year beginning after , by not later than the date that is the twenty-first day of that fiscal year, the Secretary shall submit, by not later than that date, to the Committee on Transportation and Infrastructure of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Environment and Public Works of the Senate, a written statement of the reason for not making the apportionment in a timely manner.September 30, 1998

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: The calculation of official apportionments of funds to the States under this title is a primary responsibility of the Department and shall be carried out only by employees (and not contractors) of the Department.

children.heading: .—Prohibition on use of funds to hire contractors
children.label: B .—Prohibition on use of funds to hire contractors
children.text: None of the funds made available under this title shall be used to hire contractors to calculate the apportionments of funds to States.

children.heading: .—Transfer of Highway and Transit Funds
children.label: f .—Transfer of Highway and Transit Funds

children.heading: .—Transfer of highway funds for transit projects
children.label: 1 .—Transfer of highway funds for transit projects

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), amounts made available for transit projects or transportation planning under this title may be transferred to and administered by the Secretary in accordance with chapter 53 of title 49.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: B .—Non-federal share
children.text: The provisions of this title relating to the non-Federal share shall apply to the amounts transferred under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Transfer of transit funds for highway projects
children.label: 2 .—Transfer of transit funds for highway projects

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), amounts made available for highway projects or transportation planning under chapter 53 of title 49 may be transferred to and administered by the Secretary in accordance with this title.

children.heading: .—Non-federal share
children.label: B .—Non-federal share
children.text: The provisions of chapter 53 of title 49 relating to the non-Federal share shall apply to amounts transferred under subparagraph (A).

children.heading: .—Transfer of funds among states or to an operating administration of the department of transportation
children.label: 3 .—Transfer of funds among states or to an operating administration of the department of transportation

children.heading: .—In general
children.label: A .—In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the Secretary may, at the request of a State, transfer amounts apportioned or allocated under this title to the State to another State, or to an operating administration of the Department of Transportation, for the purpose of funding 1 or more projects that are eligible for assistance with amounts so apportioned or allocated.

children.heading: .—Apportionment
children.label: B .—Apportionment
children.text: The transfer shall have no effect on any apportionment of amounts to a State under this section.

children.heading: .—Funds suballocated to urbanized areas
children.label: C .—Funds suballocated to urbanized areas
children.text: Amounts that are apportioned or allocated to a State under subsection (b)(3) (as in effect on the day before the date of enactment of the MAP–21) or subsection (b)(2) and attributed to an urbanized area of a State with a population of more than 200,000 individuals under section 133(d) may be transferred under this paragraph only if the metropolitan planning organization designated for the area concurs, in writing, with the transfer request.

children.heading: .—Transfer of obligation authority
children.label: 4 .—Transfer of obligation authority
children.text: Obligation authority for amounts transferred under this subsection shall be transferred in the same manner and amount as the amounts for the projects that are transferred under this section.

children.heading: Highway Trust Fund Transparency and Accountability Reports.—
children.label: g Highway Trust Fund Transparency and Accountability Reports.—

children.heading: .—Compilation of data
children.label: 1 .—Compilation of data
children.text: Not later than 180 days after the date of enactment of the FAST Act, the Secretary shall compile data in accordance with this subsection on the use of Federal-aid highway funds made available under this title.

children.heading: .—Requirements
children.label: 2 .—Requirements
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that the reports required under this subsection are made available in a user-friendly manner on the public Internet website of the Department of Transportation and can be searched and downloaded by users of the website.

children.heading: .—Base Apportionment Defined
children.label: h .—Base Apportionment Defined
children.text: In this section, the term “base apportionment” means the combined amount authorized for appropriation for the national highway performance program under section 119, the surface transportation block grant program under section 133, the highway safety improvement program under section 148, the congestion mitigation and air quality improvement program under section 149, the national highway freight program under section 167, the carbon reduction program under section 175, to carry out subsection (c) of the PROTECT program under section 176, and to carry out section 134.
